props.caption: The governed pause: 104 matches, two engineered interrupts each, and a fight over who fills them

items.note: China's coast-guard patrol east of Taiwan entered its second month with a crew rotation: the Xiushan task group replaced Daishan on 4 July, and Taiwan tracked two cutters 54 nautical miles off Hualien. National Security Council chief Joseph Wu counted a record 110-plus Chinese navy and coast-guard vessels across the First Island Chain.

Crude sits in contango while Russian motorists queue: Novorossiysk suspended gasoline sales, Anapa caps fills at 20 litres, and Moscow arranged Indian gasoline imports with plans reported up to 400,000 tonnes a month. Putin signed fuel-market tax changes into law on 4 July.

Every World Cup match now carries a mandatory three-minute break in each half, framed as player welfare. Trade estimates price the new ad inventory in the hundreds of millions; Telemundo keeps its feed live through the pauses and says viewers stay. FIFA's public rulebook still reads weather-contingent.

A Pentagon memo put one office over unmanned systems and their acquisition marketplaces, with power to block catalogs it hasn't approved. The same week, Microsoft warned that agent tool descriptions behave like system prompts, and two CVSS-9.8 zero-click flaws in a coding agent's tool path were disclosed, patched since April.

items.what: A Russian missile struck a Kyiv high-rise in the week's deadliest attack on the capital, with local officials reporting about thirty dead; Ukraine's air force logged 322 air-attack means overnight into 5 July.
Insurgents pressed a second day of multi-axis attacks in Mali, and OSINT accounts tracked a Malian army and Africa Corps convoy blocked between Tarkint and Tabankort on its way to relieve Anefis.
The UN human-rights chief warned that a catastrophe is unfolding around El Obeid, citing fifteen drone strikes on the besieged North Kordofan capital in June and atrocity risk on the roads out.
Electoral authorities certified Keiko Fujimori's runoff win by roughly 50,000 votes out of 18 million; Roberto Sánchez refuses to recognise the result ahead of the 28 July inauguration.
The death toll from the coastal earthquakes rose to 2,954 as rescue work continued in La Guaira, making the sequence Latin America's deadliest disaster in years.
The DRC outbreak reached 1,502 cases and 473 deaths by 3 July as the first Bundibugyo treatment trial enrolled patients and an imported case surfaced in France.
